source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Welding Flash Fire Incident

event.top_event: Welding flash fire with personnel injury
actions_taken: Fire Watch used 20 lb dry chemical extinguisher to sweep top of sump pile
Fire Watch expelled remaining extinguisher content directly inside sump
Fellow workers tossed life ring and life vest to injured worker
Welder A entered water and assisted injured worker back to platform
Both workers brought to site paramedic for evaluation
Welder B transferred to shore for treatment
Sump pile was pumped out and cleaned to eliminate hazardous vapors
event.summary: At 13:50 hours on August 10, 2017, a welding flash fire occurred at the +12 level of EB-110A platform during hot work to prepare for temporary work platform installation. A welder using an oxygen/acetylene cutting torch to shape angle-iron on the B3 piling directed the torch toward an open out-of-service sump pile containing hydrocarbon fluids. The torch ignited vapors from the sump, causing a flash fire. One welder (Welder B) sustained burns to hands and fell approximately 12 feet into the water. He was retrieved and transferred to UTMB Hospital. The fire self-extinguished. No environmental impact or additional injuries occurred.
recommendations: Sump pile pumped out and cleaned to eliminate hazardous vapors prior to hot work
Construction Site Supervisor to personally verify Hot Work Permit controls adherence prior to authorization
Safety stand down meeting to reiterate task-specific JSA use, Stop Work Authority, and communication
Contracted construction supervisors to be personally involved in task planning, hazard identification, and mitigation verification
Contracted Safety Representation assigned to support JSA, permit control, and hazard identification processes
Incident awareness communicated to all similar operations
Incident Safety Alert issued to all personnel including contractors
Refresher training provided to Site Supervisors on Hot Work Permit controls
Awareness training in hazard identification provided to contracted construction and cleaning services
All contracted personnel trained and verified as competent in gas detection use
key_phrases: failure to remove flammable substances within 35 feet radius

hazards.name: Hot work operations with ignition sources
Open sump pile containing flammable fluids

threats.name: Failure to isolate or remove flammable substances within hot work radius
Inadequate gas detection and monitoring of sump pile
Torch slag or sparks directed toward sump pile
Sump pile lid left open exposing hydrocarbon vapors

supporting_text: A contracted Fire Watch worker stated he bump tested the gas detector and took samples at various intended hot work areas (B2, A1 and B3 piling). All areas indicated 0% LEL
At 10:30 hours on 10 August 2017, the sump pile was checked again by the firewatch for LEL (0% LEL detected). The firewatch did not question why the sump pile surface lid was open
At 13:40 hours on 10 August 2017, two welders began installing angle-iron. Gas sampling was again performed on top and inside of the Sump Pile by the firewatch. The results were 0% LEL

controls.name: Hot Work Permit system
controls.barrier_role: Authorization and control of hot work activities
controls.lod_basis: Procedural control to ensure hazard assessment before hot work

supporting_text: The JSA did not include specific hazard identification and/or mitigation of hot work in close proximity to the Sump Pile, which contained hydrocarbon fluids
Failure to remove (or shield) flammable substances within 35' radius of the work area prior to starting hot work (welding) operations

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA)
controls.lod_basis: Pre-work hazard assessment and control planning

supporting_text: The JSA did not include specific hazard identification and/or mitigation of hot work in close proximity to the Sump Pile, which contained hydrocarbon fluids

controls.name: Sump pile isolation and containment
controls.lod_basis: Removal or shielding of ignition sources within 35 feet

supporting_text: The welder does not reinstall the lid and leaves a 1" to 2" opening (slid over to one side)
The supervisor then left the area, believing the welder would re-install the top lid with new bolts and nuts
The firewatch did not question why the sump pile surface lid was open

controls.name: Supervision and work oversight
controls.barrier_role: Real-time monitoring and enforcement of controls
controls.lod_basis: Supervisory verification of permit compliance and hazard mitigation

supporting_text: At 13:30 hours on 10 August 2017, Construction Project Site Supervisor requested and witnessed the firewatch rechecking the process piping and sump pile at the B3 pilling (0% LEL) before hotwork began in the area. This observation was made from the top of the stairway. He did not identify the opened Sump Pile lid at this time

controls.name: Fire Watch and emergency response
controls.lod_basis: Immediate fire detection and suppression capability

supporting_text: The Fire Watch (using a 20 lb. dry chemical extinguisher) swept the top of the Sump Pile with dry chemical and then expelled the remaining content of the extinguisher directly inside the sump
it was a flash fire, fire went out

controls.name: Personnel rescue and emergency procedures
controls.barrier_role: Rescue and medical response to personnel injury

supporting_text: Fellow workers tossed a life ring and life vest to Welder B
Welder A removed his boots, entered the water and swam to Welder B with a life ring and tethered line and successfully assisted him back to the platform
Both men were brought to the site paramedic for evaluation. Welder B was transferred to shore for treatment

controls.name: Communication and coordination between work teams
controls.barrier_role: Ensure shared understanding of work scope and hazards
controls.lod_basis: Clear communication of work plans and hazard status

supporting_text: The supervisor then left the area, believing the welder would re-install the top lid with new bolts and nuts. The welder does not reinstall the lid and leaves a 1" to 2" opening (slid over to one side). He stated that he believed the Supervisor was going to retrieve a bucket to put the fluid soaked cloth into

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
